page: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several categories. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores rank in the top 10%, alongside top-tier results in static web serving throughput and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top 10%, while large block read and write bandwidths fall into the top 25%. For compression and decompression tasks, the server achieves top-tier single-threaded gzip and both single- and multi-threaded bzip3 speeds.

This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, web serving, software compilation, and text processing. It supports advanced vector instructions such as AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI, which assist in processing small-to-medium language model inference tasks, where it achieves top-tier text generation speeds for 135M and 7B models. However, for larger 70B models, inference performance drops to average levels. The baseline network bandwidth is 15 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The server utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, offers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution, database operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference. Conversely, performance for large-scale 70B LLM inference is average. This server is optimized for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database management, software compilation, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 15 Gbps baseline network speed, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and database operation benchmarks
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for web serving, database management, and software compilation
